clinical_history: Which ONE of the following abnormalities is not present in the following ECG?
diagnosis: e) AV Wenckebach. The rhythm is sinus with premature atrial complexes (PAC's), accounting for the slight pauses. The biphasic P waves in lead V1 with broad negative component are indicative of left atrial abnormality. The Q waves in lead II, III and AVF are due to prior myocardial infarction with associated nonspecific ST-T abnormalities in the inferolateral leads. AV Wenckebach with progressive PR prolongation and non-conducted sinus P waves is not seen.
